<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="512" fill="#0b1020"/>

  <!-- Desk glow -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="438" rx="170" ry="28" fill="#14224a"/>

  <!-- Monitor body -->
  <rect x="118" y="118" width="276" height="238" rx="16" fill="#121a34" stroke="#2a3a6a" stroke-width="6"/>
  <rect x="148" y="142" width="216" height="176" rx="12" fill="#0e1630" stroke="#3b5aa0" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Abstract code lines (no letters) -->
  <g fill="#9ad0ff" opacity="0.95">
    <rect x="165" y="176" width="178" height="10" rx="5"/>
    <rect x="165" y="202" width="120" height="10" rx="5"/>
    <rect x="165" y="228" width="160" height="10" rx="5"/>
    <rect x="165" y="254" width="132" height="10" rx="5"/>
    <rect x="165" y="280" width="170" height="10" rx="5"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Bracket-like shapes -->
  <g stroke="#7bd3ff" stroke-width="8" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round">
    <path d="M176 166 C160 176 160 188 176 198 C160 208 160 220 176 230"/>
    <path d="M336 166 C352 176 352 188 336 198 C352 208 352 220 336 230"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Stand -->
  <rect x="232" y="356" width="48" height="36" rx="8" fill="#121a34" stroke="#2a3a6a" stroke-width="6"/>
  <rect x="210" y="392" width="92" height="22" rx="10" fill="#0f1730" stroke="#2a3a6a" stroke-width="6"/>

  <!-- Gear (programming/engineering) -->
  <g transform="translate(370,215) rotate(10)">
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="44" fill="#0f1730" stroke="#3b5aa0" stroke-width="6"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="18" fill="#121a34" stroke="#7bd3ff" stroke-width="5"/>
    <!-- teeth -->
    <g fill="#0f1730" stroke="#3b5aa0" stroke-width="5">
      <rect x="-8" y="-63" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(0)"/>
      <rect x="-8" y="37" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(180)"/>
      <rect x="-63" y="-8" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(-90)"/>
      <rect x="37" y="-8" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(90)"/>
      <rect x="-8" y="-63" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(45)"/>
      <rect x="-8" y="37" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(-135)"/>
      <rect x="-63" y="-8" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(-45)"/>
      <rect x="37" y="-8" width="16" height="26" rx="7" transform="rotate(135)"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Flow arrows around monitor -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#7bd3ff" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" opacity="0.95">
    <path d="M140 318 C95 306 78 270 103 244"/>
    <path d="M140 318 C120 340 86 340 68 314" stroke-opacity="0.35"/>
    <path d="M103 244 L123 245 L114 228"/>

    <path d="M372 300 C415 292 434 256 408 230"/>
    <path d="M372 300 C396 320 430 316 446 288" stroke-opacity="0.35"/>
    <path d="M408 230 L390 234 L400 216"/>

    <path d="M208 108 C218 78 248 64 280 72"/>
    <path d="M208 108 C230 120 252 120 272 110" stroke-opacity="0.35"/>
    <path d="M280 72 L274 56 L260 68"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Small spark dots -->
  <g fill="#9ad0ff" opacity="0.9">
    <circle cx="110" cy="280" r="5"/>
    <circle cx="410" cy="265" r="5"/>
    <circle cx="300" cy="110" r="5"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Visualizing programming concepts**

I need to design a concept for programming without using any letters. For the one-word guessing game, maybe I could draw a computer with code brackets but no text involved. I'll create a generic code window with curly braces and flowcharts instead. The word is "programming," so I'll represent it with a monitor featuring lines, brackets, and flow arrows.

I'll keep the design simple and focus on a light background with abstract shapes representing code, like braces and horizontal lines, while ensuring that I avoid text entirely. I’ll return this as JSON.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
